sectiondef.header: Callback identifiers used as parameters to XCanPs_SetHandler()

briefdescription.para: Handler type for frame sending interrupt.
Handler type for frame reception interrupt.
Handler type for all other interrupts.

sectiondef.header: Register offsets for the CAN. Each register is 32 bits.

briefdescription.para: TX High Priority Buf Data Word 2.

sectiondef.header: Software Reset Register (SRR) Bit Definitions and Masks
Mode Select Register (MSR) Bit Definitions and Masks
Baud Rate Prescaler register (BRPR) Bit Definitions and Masks
Bit Timing Register (BTR) Bit Definitions and Masks
Error Counter Register (ECR) Bit Definitions and Masks
Error Status Register (ESR) Bit Definitions and Masks
Status Register (SR) Bit Definitions and Masks
Interrupt Status/Enable/Clear Register Bit Definitions and Masks
CAN Timestamp Control Register (TCR) Bit Definitions and Masks
CAN Watermark Register (WIR) Bit Definitions and Masks
CAN Frame Identifier (TX High Priority Buffer/TX/RX/Acceptance Filter
CAN Frame Data Length Code (TX High Priority Buffer/TX/RX)
CAN Frame Data Word 1 (TX High Priority Buffer/TX/RX)
CAN Frame Data Word 2 (TX High Priority Buffer/TX/RX)
Acceptance Filter Register (AFR) Bit Definitions and Masks

briefdescription.para: Maximum CAN frame length in bytes.
Callback type for frame sending and reception interrupts.

parameterdescription.para: is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked.
is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked.
is a bit mask indicating the cause of the error. Its value equals 'OR'ing one or more XCANPS_ESR_* values defined in xcanps_hw.h

briefdescription.para: Callback type for all kinds of interrupts except sending frame interrupt, receiving frame interrupt, and error interrupt.

parameterdescription.para: is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the callback functions, and passed back to the upper layer when the callback is invoked.
is a bit mask indicating the pending interrupts. Its value equals 'OR'ing one or more XCANPS_IXR_* defined in xcanps_hw.h

briefdescription.para: This table contains configuration information for each CAN device in the system.
This table contains configuration information for each CAN device in the system.
Buffers to hold frames to send and receive.

detaileddescription.para: These are declared as global so that they are not on the stack.Txframe buffer

briefdescription.para: This routine is a stub for the asynchronous callbacks.

detaileddescription.para: The stub is here in case the upper layer forgot to set the handler(s). On initialization, all handlers are set to this callback. It is considered an error for this handler to be invoked.

briefdescription.para: This function initializes a XCanPs instance/driver.

detaileddescription.para:
The initialization entails:
Initialize all members of the XCanPs structure.
Reset the CAN device. The CAN device will enter Configuration Mode immediately after the reset is finished.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanPs instance.
points to the XCanPs device configuration structure.

is the device base address in the virtual memory address space. If the address translation is not used then the physical address is passed. Unexpected errors may occur if the address mapping is changed after this function is invoked.

briefdescription.para: This function resets the CAN device.

detaileddescription.para: Calling this function resets the device immediately, and any pending transmission or reception is terminated at once. Both Object Layer and Transfer Layer are reset. This function does not reset the Physical Layer. All registers are reset to the default values, and no previous status will be restored. TX FIFO, RX FIFO and TX High Priority Buffer are also reset.

When a reset is required due to an internal error, the driver notifies the upper layer software of this need through the error status code or interrupts. The upper layer software is responsible for calling this Reset function and then re-configuring the device.
The CAN device will be in Configuration Mode immediately after this function returns.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanPs instance.

briefdescription.para: This routine returns the current operation mode of the CAN device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanPs instance.

listitem.para: XCANPS_MODE_CONFIG if the device is in Configuration Mode.
XCANPS_MODE_SLEEP if the device is in Sleep Mode.
XCANPS_MODE_NORMAL if the device is in Normal Mode.
XCANPS_MODE_LOOPBACK if the device is in Loop Back Mode.
XCANPS_MODE_SNOOP if the device is in Snoop Mode.

briefdescription.para: This function allows the CAN device to enter one of the following operation modes:

para.verbatim: - Configuration Mode: Pass in parameter XCANPS_MODE_CONFIG - Sleep Mode: Pass in parameter XCANPS_MODE_SLEEP - Normal Mode: Pass in parameter XCANPS_MODE_NORMAL - Loop Back Mode: Pass in parameter XCANPS_MODE_LOOPBACK. - Snoop Mode: Pass in parameter XCANPS_MODE_SNOOP.

detaileddescription.para: Read the xcanps.h file and device specification for detailed description of each operation mode.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XCanPs instance.
OperationMode
specify which operation mode to enter. Valid value is any of XCANPS_MODE_* defined in xcanps.h. Multiple modes can not be entered at the same time.
None.
This function does NOT ensure CAN device enters the specified operation mode before it returns the control to the caller. The caller is responsible for checking current operation mode using XCanPs_GetMode().

briefdescription.para: This function returns Status value from Status Register (SR).

detaileddescription.para: Use the XCANPS_SR_* constants defined in xcanps_hw.h to interpret the returned value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanPs instance.

simplesect.para: The 32-bit value read from Status Register.

briefdescription.para: This function reads Receive and Transmit error counters.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanPs instance.
is a pointer to data in which the Receive Error counter value is returned.
is a pointer to data in which the Transmit Error counter value is returned.

briefdescription.para: This function reads Error Status value from Error Status Register (ESR).

detaileddescription.para: Use the XCANPS_ESR_* constants defined in xcanps_hw.h to interpret the returned value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanPs instance.

simplesect.para: The 32-bit value read from Error Status Register.

briefdescription.para: This function clears Error Status bit(s) previously set in Error Status Register (ESR).

detaileddescription.para: Use the XCANPS_ESR_* constants defined in xcanps_hw.h to create the value to pass in. If a bit was cleared in Error Status Register before this function is called, it will not be modified.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanPs instance.
is he 32-bit mask used to clear bits in Error Status Register. Multiple XCANPS_ESR_* values can be 'OR'ed to clear multiple bits.

briefdescription.para: This function sends a CAN Frame.

detaileddescription.para: If the TX FIFO is not full then the given frame is written into the the TX FIFO otherwise, it returns an error code immediately. This function does not wait for the given frame being sent to CAN bus.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanPs instance.
is a pointer to a 32-bit aligned buffer containing the CAN frame to be sent.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if TX FIFO was not full and the given frame was written into the FIFO.
XST_FIFO_NO_ROOM if there is no room in the TX FIFO for the given frame.
XST_DEVICE_BUSY if a transfer is already in progress.

briefdescription.para: This function receives a CAN Frame.

detaileddescription.para: This function first checks if RX FIFO is empty, if not, it then reads a frame from the RX FIFO into the given buffer. This function returns error code immediately if there is no frame in the RX FIFO.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanPs instance.
is a pointer to a 32-bit aligned buffer where the CAN frame to be written.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if RX FIFO was not empty and a frame was read from RX FIFO successfully and written into the given buffer.
XST_NO_DATA if there is no frame to be received from the FIFO.

briefdescription.para: This routine sends a CAN High Priority frame.

detaileddescription.para: This function first checks if TX High Priority Buffer is empty. If yes, it then writes the given frame into the Buffer. If not, this function returns immediately. This function does not wait for the given frame being sent to CAN bus.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XCanPs instance.
FramePtr
is a pointer to a 32-bit aligned buffer containing the CAN High Priority frame to be sent.
XST_SUCCESS if TX High Priority Buffer was not full and the given frame was written into the buffer.
XST_FIFO_NO_ROOM if there is no room in the TX High Priority Buffer for this frame.
XST_DEVICE_BUSY if a transfer is already in progress.
If the frame needs to be sent immediately and not delayed by processor's interrupt handling, the caller should disable interrupt at processor level before invoking this function.

briefdescription.para: This routine enables individual acceptance filters.

detaileddescription.para: Up to 4 filters could be enabled.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanPs instance.

specifies which filter(s) to enable. Use any XCANPS_AFR_UAF*_MASK to enable one filter, and "Or" multiple XCANPS_AFR_UAF*_MASK values if multiple filters need to be enabled. Any filter not specified in this parameter will keep its previous enable/disable setting.

briefdescription.para: This routine disables individual acceptance filters.

detaileddescription.para: Up to 4 filters could be disabled. If all acceptance filters are disabled then all the received frames are stored in the RX FIFO.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanPs instance.

specifies which filter(s) to disable. Use any XCANPS_AFR_UAF*_MASK to disable one filter, and "Or" multiple XCANPS_AFR_UAF*_MASK values if multiple filters need to be disabled. Any filter not specified in this parameter will keep its previous enable/disable setting. If all acceptance filters are disabled then all received frames are stored in the RX FIFO.

briefdescription.para: This function returns enabled acceptance filters.

detaileddescription.para: Use XCANPS_AFR_UAF*_MASK defined in xcanps_hw.h to interpret the returned value. If no acceptance filters are enabled then all received frames are stored in the RX FIFO.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanPs instance.

simplesect.para: The value stored in Acceptance Filter Register.

memberdef.argsstring: (XCanPs *InstancePtr, u32 FilterIndex, u32 MaskValue, u32 IdValue)
memberdef.name: XCanPs_AcceptFilterSet

briefdescription.para: This function sets values to the Acceptance Filter Mask Register (AFMR) and Acceptance Filter ID Register (AFIR) for the specified Acceptance Filter.

detaileddescription.para: Use XCANPS_IDR_* defined in xcanps_hw.h to create the values to set the filter. Read the xcanps.h file and device specification for details.

This function should be called only after:
The given filter is disabled by calling XCanPs_AcceptFilterDisable()
And the CAN device is ready to accept writes to AFMR and AFIR, i.e., XCanPs_IsAcceptFilterBusy() returns FALSE.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanPs instance.
defines which Acceptance Filter Mask and ID Register to set. Use any single XCANPS_AFR_UAF*_MASK value.
is the value to write to the chosen Acceptance Filter Mask Register.
is the value to write to the chosen Acceptance Filter ID Register.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the values were set successfully.
XST_FAILURE if the given filter was not disabled, or the CAN device was not ready to accept writes to AFMR and AFIR.

briefdescription.para: This function reads the values of the Acceptance Filter Mask and ID Register for the specified Acceptance Filter.

detaileddescription.para: Use XCANPS_IDR_* defined in xcanps_hw.h to interpret the values. Read the xcanps.h file and device specification for details.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanPs instance.
defines which Acceptance Filter Mask Register to get Mask and ID from. Use any single XCANPS_FILTER_* value.
is a pointer to the data in which the Mask value read from the chosen Acceptance Filter Mask Register is returned.
is a pointer to the data in which the ID value read from the chosen Acceptance Filter ID Register is returned.

briefdescription.para: This routine sets Baud Rate Prescaler value.

detaileddescription.para: The system clock for the CAN controller is divided by (Prescaler + 1) to generate the quantum clock needed for sampling and synchronization. Read the device specification for details.
Baud Rate Prescaler can be set only if the CAN device is in Configuration Mode. Call XCanPs_EnterMode() to enter Configuration Mode before using this function.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanPs instance.
is the value to set. Valid values are from 0 to 255.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the Baud Rate Prescaler value is set successfully.
XST_FAILURE if CAN device is not in Configuration Mode.

briefdescription.para: This routine gets Baud Rate Prescaler value.

detaileddescription.para: The system clock for the CAN controller is divided by (Prescaler + 1) to generate the quantum clock needed for sampling and synchronization. Read the device specification for details.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanPs instance.

simplesect.para: Current used Baud Rate Prescaler value. The value's range is from 0 to 255.

memberdef.argsstring: (XCanPs *InstancePtr, u8 SyncJumpWidth, u8 TimeSegment2, u8 TimeSegment1)
memberdef.name: XCanPs_SetBitTiming

detaileddescription.para: Time segment 1, Time segment 2 and Synchronization Jump Width are set in this function. Device specification requires the values passed into this function be one less than the actual values of these fields. Read the device specification for details.
Bit time can be set only if the CAN device is in Configuration Mode. Call XCanPs_EnterMode() to enter Configuration Mode before using this function.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanPs instance.
is the Synchronization Jump Width value to set. Valid values are from 0 to 3.
is the Time Segment 2 value to set. Valid values are from 0 to 7.
is the Time Segment 1 value to set. Valid values are from 0 to 15.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS if the Bit time is set successfully.
XST_FAILURE if CAN device is not in Configuration Mode.

detaileddescription.para: Time segment 1, Time segment 2 and Synchronization Jump Width values are read in this function. According to device specification, the actual value of each of these fields is one more than the value read. Read the device specification for details.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanPs instance.
will store the Synchronization Jump Width value after this function returns. Its value ranges from 0 to 3.
will store the Time Segment 2 value after this function returns. Its value ranges from 0 to 7.
will store the Time Segment 1 value after this function returns. Its value ranges from 0 to 15.

briefdescription.para: This routine sets the Rx Full threshold in the Watermark Interrupt Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanPs instance.
is the threshold to be set. The valid values are from 1 to 63.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE - If the CAN device is not in Configuration Mode.
XST_SUCCESS - If the Rx Full threshold is set in Watermark Interrupt Register.

simplesect.para: The threshold can only be set when the CAN device is in the configuration mode.

briefdescription.para: This routine gets the Rx Full threshold from the Watermark Interrupt Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanPs instance.

simplesect.para: The Rx FIFO full watermark threshold value. The valid values are 1 to 63.

briefdescription.para: This routine sets the Tx Empty Threshold in the Watermark Interrupt Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanPs instance.
is the threshold to be set. The valid values are from 1 to 63.

listitem.para: XST_FAILURE - If the CAN device is not in Configuration Mode.
XST_SUCCESS - If the threshold is set in Watermark Interrupt Register.

simplesect.para: The threshold can only be set when the CAN device is in the configuration mode.

briefdescription.para: This routine gets the Tx Empty threshold from Watermark Interrupt Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanPs instance.

simplesect.para: The Tx Empty FIFO threshold value. The valid values are 1 to 63.

briefdescription.para: This function runs a self-test on the CAN driver/device.

detaileddescription.para: The test resets the device, sets up the Loop Back mode, sends a standard frame, receives the frame, verifies the contents, and resets the device again.
Note that this is a destructive test in that resets of the device are performed. Refer the device specification for the device status after the reset operation.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XCanPs instance.
XST_SUCCESS if the self-test passed. i.e., the frame received via the internal loop back has the same contents as the frame sent.
XST_FAILURE Otherwise.
If the CAN device does not work properly, this function may enter an infinite loop and will never return to the caller.
If XST_FAILURE is returned, the device is not reset so that the caller could have a chance to check reason(s) causing the failure.
Use the XCANPS_IXR_* constants defined in xcanps_hw.h to create the bit-mask to enable interrupts.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanPs instance.
is the mask to enable. Bit positions of 1 will be enabled. Bit positions of 0 will keep the previous setting. This mask is formed by OR'ing XCANPS_IXR_* bits defined in xcanps_hw.h.

detaileddescription.para: Use the XCANPS_IXR_* constants defined in xcanps_hw.h to create the bit-mask to disable interrupt(s).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanPs instance.
is the mask to disable. Bit positions of 1 will be disabled. Bit positions of 0 will keep the previous setting. This mask is formed by OR'ing XCANPS_IXR_* bits defined in xcanps_hw.h.

detaileddescription.para: Use the XCANPS_IXR_* constants defined in xcanps_hw.h to interpret the returned value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanPs instance.

briefdescription.para: This routine returns interrupt status read from Interrupt Status Register.

detaileddescription.para: Use the XCANPS_IXR_* constants defined in xcanps_hw.h to interpret the returned value.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanPs instance.

simplesect.para: The value stored in Interrupt Status Register.

detaileddescription.para: Every bit set in Interrupt Status Register indicates that a specific type of interrupt is occurring, and this function clears one or more interrupts by writing a bit mask to Interrupt Clear Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanPs instance.
is the mask to clear. Bit positions of 1 will be cleared. Bit positions of 0 will not change the previous interrupt status. This mask is formed by OR'ing XCANPS_IXR_* bits defined in xcanps_hw.h.

briefdescription.para: This routine is the interrupt handler for the CAN driver.

detaileddescription.para: This handler reads the interrupt status from the ISR, determines the source of the interrupts, calls according callbacks, and finally clears the interrupts.

Application beyond this driver is responsible for providing callbacks to handle interrupts and installing the callbacks using XCanPs_SetHandler() during initialization phase. An example delivered with this driver demonstrates how this could be done.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanPs instance that just interrupted.

briefdescription.para: This routine installs an asynchronous callback function for the given HandlerType:

para.preformatted: HandlerType Callback Function Type ----------------------- ------------------------ XCANPS_HANDLER_SEND XCanPs_SendRecvHandler XCANPS_HANDLER_RECV XCanPs_SendRecvHandler XCANPS_HANDLER_ERROR XCanPs_ErrorHandler XCANPS_HANDLER_EVENT XCanPs_EventHandler

HandlerType Invoked by this driver when: ------------------------------------------------------------------------- XCANPS_HANDLER_SEND A frame transmitted by a call to XCanPs_Send() has been sent successfully.
XCANPS_HANDLER_RECV A frame(s) has been received and is sitting in the RX FIFO.
XCANPS_HANDLER_ERROR An error interrupt is occurring.
XCANPS_HANDLER_EVENT Any other kind of interrupt is occurring.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanPs instance.
specifies which handler is to be attached.
is the address of the callback function.
is a user data item that will be passed to the callback function when it is invoked.

listitem.para: XST_SUCCESS when handler is installed.
XST_INVALID_PARAM when HandlerType is invalid.

simplesect.para: Invoking this function for a handler that already has been installed replaces it with the new handler.

briefdescription.para: Looks up the device configuration based on the unique device ID/BaseAddress.

detaileddescription.para: The XCanPs_ConfigTable[] contains the configuration info for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration found or NULL if the specified device ID/BaseAddress was not found. See xcanps.h for the definition of XCanPs_Config.
In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.

briefdescription.para: This function resets the CAN device.

detaileddescription.para: Calling this function resets the device immediately, and any pending transmission or reception is terminated at once. Both Object Layer and Transfer Layer are reset. This function does not reset the Physical Layer. All registers are reset to the default values, and no previous status will be restored. TX FIFO, RX FIFO and TX High Priority Buffer are also reset.
The CAN device will be in Configuration Mode immediately after this function returns.

parameterdescription.para: is the baseaddress of the interface.

briefdescription.para: This macro checks if the transmission is complete.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanPs instance.

listitem.para: TRUE if the transmission is done.
FALSE if the transmission is not done.

briefdescription.para: This macro checks if the transmission FIFO is full.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanPs instance.

listitem.para: TRUE if TX FIFO is full.
FALSE if the TX FIFO is NOT full.

briefdescription.para: This macro checks if the Transmission High Priority Buffer is full.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanPs instance.

listitem.para: TRUE if the TX High Priority Buffer is full.
FALSE if the TX High Priority Buffer is NOT full.

briefdescription.para: This macro checks if the receive FIFO is empty.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanPs instance.

listitem.para: TRUE if RX FIFO is empty.
FALSE if the RX FIFO is NOT empty.

briefdescription.para: This macro checks if the CAN device is ready for the driver to change Acceptance Filter Identifier Registers (AFIR) and Acceptance Filter Mask Registers (AFMR).

detaileddescription.para: AFIR and AFMR for a filter are changeable only after the filter is disabled and this routine returns FALSE. The filter can be disabled using the XCanPs_AcceptFilterDisable function.
Use the XCanPs_Accept_* functions for configuring the acceptance filters.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanPs instance.

listitem.para: TRUE if the device is busy and NOT ready to accept writes to AFIR and AFMR.
FALSE if the device is ready to accept writes to AFIR and AFMR.

memberdef.name: XCanPs_CreateIdValue
memberdef.initializer: ((((StandardId) << XCANPS_IDR_ID1_SHIFT) & XCANPS_IDR_ID1_MASK) | \ (((SubRemoteTransReq) << XCANPS_IDR_SRR_SHIFT) & XCANPS_IDR_SRR_MASK)|\ (((IdExtension) << XCANPS_IDR_IDE_SHIFT) & XCANPS_IDR_IDE_MASK) | \ (((ExtendedId) << XCANPS_IDR_ID2_SHIFT) & XCANPS_IDR_ID2_MASK) | \ ((RemoteTransReq) & XCANPS_IDR_RTR_MASK))

briefdescription.para: This macro calculates CAN message identifier value given identifier field values.

detaileddescription.para:
StandardId
contains Standard Message ID value.
SubRemoteTransReq
contains Substitute Remote Transmission Request value.
IdExtension
contains Identifier Extension value.
ExtendedId
contains Extended Message ID value.
RemoteTransReq
contains Remote Transmission Request value.
Message Identifier value.
C-Style signature: u32 XCanPs_CreateIdValue(u32 StandardId, u32 SubRemoteTransReq, u32 IdExtension, u32 ExtendedId, u32 RemoteTransReq) Read the CAN specification for meaning of each parameter.

briefdescription.para: This macro calculates value for Data Length Code register given Data Length Code value.

detaileddescription.para:
DataLengCode
indicates Data Length Code value.
Value that can be assigned to Data Length Code register.
C-Style signature: u32 XCanPs_CreateDlcValue(u32 DataLengCode) Read the CAN specification for meaning of Data Length Code.

briefdescription.para: This macro clears the timestamp in the Timestamp Control Register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XCanPs instance.

briefdescription.para: TX High Priority Buf Data Word 2.
This macro reads the given register.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device.
is the register offset to be read.

briefdescription.para: This macro writes the given register.

parameterdescription.para: is the base address of the device.
is the register offset to be written.
is the 32-bit value to write to the register.

briefdescription.para: Functions in this file are the minimum required functions for the XCanPs driver.

detaileddescription.para: This file contains the implementation of the XCanPs driver's static initialization functionality.
This file contains a diagnostic self-test function for the XCanPs driver.
This file contains functions related to CAN interrupt handling.
This header file contains the identifiers and basic driver functions (or macros) that can be used to access the device.
This file contains the implementation of the canps interface reset sequence.
This file contains a configuration table that specifies the configuration of CAN devices in the system.
See xcanps.h for a detailed description of the driver.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a xd/sv 01/12/10 First release 1.01a bss 12/27/11 Added the APIs XCanPs_SetTxIntrWatermark and XCanPs_GetTxIntrWatermark. 3.00 kvn 02/13/15 Modified code for MISRA-C:2012 compliance. 3.3 sne 08/06/19 Fixed coverity warnings. 3.5 sne 07/01/20 Fixed MISRAC warnings. 3.5 sne 07/01/20 Fixed multiple packets send issue. 3.7 ht 06/28/23 Added support for system device-tree flow.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a xd/sv 01/12/10 First release 2.00 hk 22/01/14 Added check for picking second instance 3.00 kvn 02/13/15 Modified code for MISRA-C:2012 compliance.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.02a adk 08/08/13 First release 3.00 kvn 02/13/15 Modified code for MISRA-C:2012 compliance. 3.5 sne 07/01/20 Fixed MISRAC warnings. 3.7 ht 06/28/23 Added support for system device-tree flow.

detaileddescription.para: Other driver functions are defined in xcanps.h.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a xd/sv 01/12/10 First release 1.01a sbs 12/27/11 Updated the Register/bit definitions Changed XCANPS_RXFWIR_RXFLL_MASK to XCANPS_WIR_FW_MASK Changed XCANPS_RXWIR_OFFSET to XCANPS_WIR_OFFSET Added XCANPS_IXR_TXFEMP_MASK for Tx Fifo Empty Changed XCANPS_IXR_RXFLL_MASK to XCANPS_IXR_RXFWMFLL_MASK Changed XCANPS_TXBUF_ID_OFFSET to XCANPS_TXHPB_ID_OFFSET XCANPS_TXBUF_DLC_OFFSET to XCANPS_TXHPB_DLC_OFFSET XCANPS_TXBUF_DW1_OFFSET to XCANPS_TXHPB_DW1_OFFSET XCANPS_TXBUF_DW2_OFFSET to XCANPS_TXHPB_DW2_OFFSET 1.02a adk 08/08/13 Updated for including the function prototype 3.00 kvn 02/13/15 Modified code for MISRA-C:2012 compliance. 3.5 sne 07/01/20 Fixed MISRAC warnings.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a xd/sv 01/12/10 First release 3.00 kvn 02/13/15 Modified code for MISRA-C:2012 compliance. 3.1 nsk 12/21/15 Updated XCanPs_IntrHandler to handle error interrupts correctly. CR#925615 3.5 sne 07/01/20 Fixed MISRAC warnings.

detaileddescription.para: Read xcanps.h file for more information.

simplesect.para: The Baud Rate Prescaler Register (BRPR) and Bit Timing Register(BTR) are setup such that CAN baud rate equals 40Kbps, given the CAN clock equal to 24MHz. These need to be changed based on the desired baudrate and CAN clock frequency.

para.preformatted:
Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a xd/sv 01/12/10 First release 2.1 adk 23/08/14 Fixed CR:798792 Peripheral test for CANPS IP in SDK claims a 40kbps baud rate but it's not. 3.00 kvn 02/13/15 Modified code for MISRA_C:2012 compliance. 3.5 sne 07/01/20 Fixed MISRAC warnings.

Ver Who Date Changes
1.00a xd/sv 01/12/10 First release 3.00 kvn 02/13/15 Modified code for MISRA-C:2012 compliance. 3.5 sne 07/01/20 Fixed MISRAC warnings. 3.7 ht 06/28/23 Added support for system device-tree flow. 3.12 vlt 12/12/25 Update Doxygen comments to include SDT flow details. 3.12 vlt 03/14/26 Updated BaseAddress type from u32 to UINTPTR to support 64-bit addressing.
